title: The SuperSXO Framework — From Search Intent to Strategic Outcome
meta_description: The SuperSXO Framework defines the seven-layer search-to-action journey: Search Intent, Visibility, Experience, Trust, Navigation, Action, Strategic Outcome.
canonical_intent: Define and own the seven-layer search-to-action framework as SuperSXO intellectual property. Establish the framework as the structural centerpiece of the asset.
summary: This page defines the SuperSXO Framework: a seven-layer model governing the complete path from search intent to strategic outcome. It is the structural core of the SXO discipline as defined by SuperSXO.com.

required_sections.name: framework_overview
required_sections.purpose: Introduce the seven-layer model as a coherent governing system, not a list of tactics.

required_sections.name: layer_search_intent
required_sections.purpose: Define Search Intent as the pre-search motivation that governs what result will satisfy the user.

required_sections.name: layer_visibility
required_sections.purpose: Define Visibility as the entry condition — necessary but not sufficient.

required_sections.name: layer_experience
required_sections.purpose: Define Experience as the quality of interaction after arrival.

required_sections.name: layer_trust
required_sections.purpose: Define Trust as the user's assessment of credibility — the most commonly missed layer in standard SEO approaches.

required_sections.name: layer_navigation
required_sections.purpose: Define Navigation as path confidence, distinct from menu design or information architecture alone.

required_sections.name: layer_action
required_sections.purpose: Define Action as the specific, measurable terminal event of the journey.

required_sections.name: layer_strategic_outcome
required_sections.purpose: Define Strategic Outcome as the downstream consequence — separating sovereign asset thinking from ordinary website thinking.

required_sections.name: framework_to_score
required_sections.purpose: Bridge from the framework explanation toward the SuperSXO Score diagnostic.

blocked_claims: The framework is the only valid SXO model.
editorial_notes: Each layer must be defined with precision. Trust is the most important layer to define carefully — it is what separates SXO from shallow SEO. The framework must feel like a governed intellectual system, not a checklist. Strategic Outcome must reinforce the sovereign asset thesis.
publication_blockers: All seven layers must have reviewed, claim-classified copy.
Route status must be 'published' in data/routes.json.
Spatial map component must be integrated into the page.
All internal links must resolve to published routes.
